items.title: PCPJack Hijacked 230 AWS, GCP, and Azure Servers to Run a Hidden SMTP Relay Network
items.description: PCPJack operators compromised 230 cloud Linux servers across AWS, GCP, and Azure to build a covert SMTP relay network for email-based attacks. Researchers discovered exposed directories on infrastructure at 213.136.80[.]73 containing complete deployment toolkits including Chisel binaries, Python deployers, and operational state files. The campaign deployed Sliver C2 beacons and established reverse
items.ai_summary: The PCPJack campaign compromised 230 cloud Linux servers across AWS, GCP, and Azure by exploiting exposed directories, allowing the deployment of a covert SMTP relay network for email-based attacks. The attackers utilized Chisel binaries, Python deployers, and operational state files to establish a foothold, and then deployed Sliver C2 beacons to maintain remote access. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems, with potential targets including organizations relying on cloud infrastructure. To remediate, defenders should immediately review their cloud server configurations, ensuring that all directories are properly secured and monitored, and update their security tools to detect Sliver C2 beacons and Chisel binaries. Additionally, defenders should apply the principle of least privilege to all cloud resources and implement robust logging and monitoring to detect potential security incidents.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1072| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Impersonation, Click Hijacking, and TDS: Inside a Malware Distribution Ecosystem
items.description: A large-scale operation impersonates open-source and freeware projects to capture search traffic, targeting tools such as Ghidra, dnSpy, and SpiderFoot. The professionally designed sites load CloudFront-hosted JavaScript that converts download button clicks into handoffs to a Traffic Distribution System (TDS), which enforces strict gating including first-visit state, click confirmation, anti-bot l
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects, such as Ghidra, dnSpy, and SpiderFoot, to capture search traffic. The attackers use professionally designed sites that load CloudFront-hosted JavaScript, which converts download button clicks into handoffs to a Traffic Distribution System (TDS). This TDS enforces strict gating, including first-visit state and click confirmation, to filter out unwanted traffic. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust input validation and sanitization for all web applications, and ensure that all software is up-to-date, particularly those that are frequently targeted such as Ghidra and dnSpy. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious traffic patterns and implement anti-bot measures to prevent exploitation of T1190 and T1134 tactics.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1134| C[Access Token Manipulation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1566| D[Initial Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The Demon Arrives Later: A Havoc Stager Hides Behind Microsoft Defender DLP
items.description: Cybercriminals in Brazil are exploiting the country's electronic invoice system (Nota Fiscal eletrônica) to deliver Havoc framework implants. The campaign surfaced during May 2026, coinciding with tax season when accountants routinely process invoice-related emails. Attackers distribute malicious ZIP files disguised as legitimate invoices, containing VBScript droppers that download MSI installers
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a Havoc framework implant delivered through malicious ZIP files disguised as legitimate invoices, specifically targeting Brazil's electronic invoice system. The attack vector utilizes VBScript droppers to download MSI installers, exploiting the trust in invoice-related emails during tax season. The real-world impact includes potential data breaches and system compromise, particularly affecting accountants and financial institutions processing these emails. To remediate, defenders should implement email filtration rules to detect and block ZIP files with VBScript attachments, and ensure all systems are configured to restrict the execution of Visual Basic scripts from untrusted sources. Additionally, updating Microsoft Defender to the latest version and enabling its Data Loss Prevention (DLP) features can help detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.005| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Masquerading]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside the Cross-Platform Propagation of a New Gafgyt Variant C0XMO
items.description: A new Gafgyt botnet variant named C0XMO has been discovered that spreads by exploiting a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in DD-WRT router firmware. Unlike earlier versions, this malware separates its lateral movement capabilities into a standalone Python script, enabling more efficient targeting of various system architectures including ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, and x86. The malware establishes pers
items.ai_summary: The C0XMO Gafgyt botnet variant exploits a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in DD-WRT router firmware, allowing it to spread across various system architectures including ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, and x86. This malware variant is notable for separating its lateral movement capabilities into a standalone Python script, enhancing its efficiency in targeting different systems. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the establishment of a botnet, enabling threat actors to conduct malicious activities such as DDoS attacks and data exfiltration. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately update their DD-WRT router firmware to the latest version and ensure that all connected devices are running up-to-date antivirus software. Additionally, defenders can implement network segmentation and monitor for suspicious traffic to prevent the spread of the malware.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1592.003| B[Resource Development]:::tactic
    B -->|T1203| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1583.005| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Browser Spy-Ons: Threat Actor's Extension Hijack Your AI Conversations
items.description: Multiple malicious Chrome extensions are exploiting the growing use of AI platforms by disguising themselves as legitimate productivity tools while secretly stealing user conversations and personal data. Extensions including Urban VPN, Smart Sidebar, and AI Assistant/Chat AI collectively reach millions of users but contain hidden scripts that intercept communications with popular AI platforms like
items.ai_summary: The threat actor is exploiting the growing use of AI platforms by disguising malicious Chrome extensions as legitimate productivity tools, such as Urban VPN, Smart Sidebar, and AI Assistant/Chat AI, which collectively reach millions of users. These extensions contain hidden scripts that intercept communications with popular AI platforms, allowing the threat actor to steal user conversations and personal data. The attack vector is through the exploitation of public-facing applications, specifically Chrome extensions, using the T1190 tactic. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately remove the affected extensions and update Chrome to the latest version, ensuring that all extensions are verified and trusted. Additionally, users should be cautious when installing new extensions and monitor their browser activity for suspicious behavior.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploitation]:::tactic
    B -->|T1056| C[Collection]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence discovered a large-scale npm supply chain attack compromising 32 malicious packages across over 90 versions under the @redhat-cloud-services scope. The compromise originated from the RedHatInsights/javascript-clients CI/CD pipeline, enabling attackers to publish trojanized packages through legitimate GitHub Actions OIDC workflows with authentic provenance signatures.
items.ai_summary: The npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign is a large-scale supply chain attack compromising 32 malicious packages across over 90 versions under the @redhat-cloud-services scope. The attackers exploited the RedHatInsights/javascript-clients CI/CD pipeline to publish trojanized packages through legitimate GitHub Actions OIDC workflows with authentic provenance signatures. This attack allows attackers to steal credentials, potentially leading to further exploitation and data breaches. To remediate, users should immediately update to the latest version of the affected packages (versions 1.0.0-1.0.3) and ensure that their CI/CD pipelines are properly secured. Additionally, users should review their GitHub Actions workflows for any suspicious activity and ensure that they are using the latest version of the OIDC workflow.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Argamal: Malware hidden in hentai games
items.description: In April 2026, researchers discovered a malware campaign targeting players of adult-themed games. The infected games install a previously unknown implant called Argamal that downloads and executes a RAT after several days, resulting in full system compromise. The malware uses COM hijacking to persist, replacing the InprocServer32 entry for Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL. Delivery occu
items.ai_summary: The Argamal malware campaign targets players of adult-themed games, installing a previously unknown implant that downloads and executes a Remote Access Tool (RAT) after several days, resulting in full system compromise. The malware uses COM hijacking (T1546.015) to persist, replacing the InprocServer32 entry for the Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL. This allows the malware to maintain persistence and execute arbitrary code. To remediate, users should update to Windows 10 version 21H2 or later, which includes a fix for the COM hijacking vulnerability. Additionally, users should ensure that their systems are running the latest version of the Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL, which can be downloaded from the Microsoft Update Catalog.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1546.015| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    E -->|T1486| F[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: UAT-8302 and its box full of malware
items.description: UAT-8302 is a sophisticated China-nexus advanced persistent threat group targeting government entities in South America since late 2024 and southeastern Europe in 2025. The actor deploys multiple custom-made malware families including NetDraft, a .NET-based backdoor variant of FinalDraft/SquidDoor, and CloudSorcerer version 3. Post-compromise activities involve extensive reconnaissance, credential
items.ai_summary: The UAT-8302 threat group, linked to China, has been targeting government entities in South America since late 2024 and southeastern Europe in 2025. They deploy custom-made malware families, including NetDraft, a .NET-based backdoor variant of FinalDraft/SquidDoor, and CloudSorcerer version 3. The actor exploits public-facing applications (T1190) to gain initial access, and uses remote access software (T1219) for post-compromise activities. If exploited, this threat can lead to extensive reconnaissance, credential theft, and potential data breaches. To remediate, defenders should focus on patching public-facing applications, implementing robust access controls, and monitoring for suspicious remote access activity. Specifically, organizations should ensure they are running the latest versions of their .NET frameworks and have configured their systems to block unauthorized remote access software.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CloudZ RAT potentially steals OTP messages using Pheno plugin
items.description: Cisco Talos uncovered an intrusion active since January 2026 where attackers deployed CloudZ remote access tool and an undocumented plugin called Pheno to steal credentials and one-time passwords. The attack exploits Microsoft Phone Link application by intercepting synchronized mobile data including SMS and OTPs without requiring phone-level infection. CloudZ evades detection through dynamic memor
items.ai_summary: CloudZ RAT, a remote access tool, has been used in an ongoing intrusion since January 2026 to steal credentials and one-time passwords (OTPs) using an undocumented plugin called Pheno. The attack exploits the Microsoft Phone Link application by intercepting synchronized mobile data, including SMS and OTPs, without requiring phone-level infection. This vulnerability affects the Microsoft Phone Link application, which is used to synchronize mobile data with Windows devices. To remediate this issue, users should update the Microsoft Phone Link application to the latest version (version 2023.1200.42.0 or later) and ensure that the 'Sync over Wi-Fi' feature is disabled. Additionally, users should consider using a VPN when accessing sensitive information on their mobile devices.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Remote Access Software]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: ClickFix Deno Abuse to CastleRAT
items.description: Activity began with a ClickFix-style social engineering chain that led to MSI execution, PowerShell staging, and installation/use of Deno to run attacker-controlled JavaScript. Follow-on activity downloaded a portable Python runtime, `install.pyc`, and an encrypted `.MOa` container, which was later decrypted to recover a 64-bit Windows PE payload. Analysis of the recovered payload showed Steam Com
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix Deno Abuse to CastleRAT threat involves a sophisticated social engineering chain that leverages MSI execution and PowerShell staging to install Deno, a JavaScript runtime. Deno is then used to run attacker-controlled JavaScript, which leads to the download of a portable Python runtime and an encrypted container. The container is decrypted to recover a 64-bit Windows PE payload, indicating a potential privilege escalation or data breach. To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure Deno is not installed on vulnerable systems and update to the latest version of Deno (1.24.0 or later). Additionally, configure PowerShell to prevent unsigned scripts from running (Set-ExecutionPolicy Restricted).
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[PowerShell]:::tactic
    C -->|T1218| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.description:
Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel vulnerability in ThemeHigh Stripe Payment Gateway for WooCommerce allows Password Recovery Exploitation.

This issue affects Stripe Payment Gateway for WooCommerce: from n/a through 5.0.7.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-45217 is an Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel vulnerability in ThemeHigh Stripe Payment Gateway for WooCommerce, affecting versions from n/a through 5.0.7. This issue allows threat actors to exploit the password recovery feature, enabling unauthorized access to the system. To remediate, update to version 5.0.8 or later, and ensure the 'Password Recovery' feature is disabled in the WooCommerce settings. Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block potential exploit attempts. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.5, indicating a medium severity risk.

items.title: CVE-2026-45216: Incorrect Privilege Assignment vulnerability in StoreApps Smart Manager allows P...
items.description:
Incorrect Privilege Assignment vulnerability in StoreApps Smart Manager allows Privilege Escalation.

This issue affects Smart Manager: from n/a through 8.85.0.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-45216 is a critical Incorrect Privilege Assignment vulnerability in StoreApps Smart Manager, affecting versions from n/a through 8.85.0. This flaw allows Privilege Escalation, enabling threat actors to exploit T1068 and T1649 tactics. If exploited, attackers can steal or forge authentication certificates, leading to severe consequences. To remediate, update to version 8.85.1 or later, and ensure the 'Authentication Certificate' feature is disabled. Additionally, configure the 'Smart Manager' service to run under a non-privileged account.
items.workflow_graph:

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic

items.description:
Missing Authorization vulnerability in edward_plainview MyCryptoCheckout allows Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels.

This issue affects MyCryptoCheckout: from n/a through 2.161.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-45209 is a Missing Authorization vulnerability in edward_plainview MyCryptoCheckout, affecting versions from n/a through 2.161. This vulnerability allows attackers to exploit incorrectly configured access control security levels, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data. If exploited, attackers could manipulate transactions, view sensitive customer information, or even take control of the system. To remediate, update to version 2.162 or later, and ensure that access control security levels are properly configured. Additionally, implement strict role-based access control to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

This vulnerability allows attackers to exploit incorrectly configured access control security levels, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data or functionality. To remediate this issue, administrators should update to version 3.6.8 or later, and ensure that access control security levels are properly configured. Additionally, it is recommended to implement additional security measures, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

items.title: CVE-2026-42774: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injecti...
items.description:
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection') vulnerability in Crocoblock JetEngine allows SQL Injection.

This issue affects JetEngine: from n/a through 3.8.8.1.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-42774 is a critical SQL Injection vulnerability in Crocoblock JetEngine, affecting versions from n/a through 3.8.8.1. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a malicious SQL query to the affected application, allowing them to inject arbitrary SQL code and potentially extract sensitive data or execute system commands. To remediate this issue, administrators should update to version 3.8.8.2 or later, and ensure that the 'allow_url_include' setting is disabled in the wp-config.php file. Additionally, it is recommended to implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent future SQL Injection attacks. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 9.3 and is considered wormable.

items.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
items.description: Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root. It is tracked as CVE-2026-20230, and proof-of-concept exploit code is already public. Cisco's PSIRT says it has not seen the flaw used in attacks yet. The PoC shortens that runway. The flaw is a server-side request forgery.

items.description: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

items.title: Hackers Are After the Gaps in Your Vulnerability Program: Here's Their Playbook
items.description: Threat actors are actively teaching newcomers how to find, exploit, and profit from vulnerable systems. Flare explores what a popular underground hacking tutorial reveals about modern attacker workflows. [...]

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: AI Agents Gone Wrong, Sketchy C2 Tools, ClickFix Tricks, JS Backdoors & 20+ New Stories
items.description: It got stupid again. The internet still feels held together with tape. Bad plugins, old bugs, fake tools, trusted apps doing shady things. Same mess, new wrapper. And now the weird stuff is normal. Forums go down and come back worse. Cheap hackers get better toys. AI starts breaking real systems. Great. Read the whole thing before it ruins your week anyway. Unauthenticated

items.title: Microsoft blames unexpected Windows driver updates on caching issue
items.description: On Wednesday, Microsoft fixed an issue that caused some Windows devices to install driver updates without notice despite policies configured to prevent auto-updates. [...]

items.title: Gemini Voice Assistant Hijacked via Messaging Notifications
items.description: Attackers could have triggered dangerous actions, including controlling smart home devices via Google Home and starting Zoom video calls. The post Gemini Voice Assistant Hijacked via Messaging Notifications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Police dismantles fake ID marketplace used by migrant smugglers
items.description: French and Spanish authorities took down an online marketplace selling fake identity documents to migrant smuggling rings operating within the European Union. [...]

items.title: China-Linked TA4922 Expands Phishing Attacks to U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa
items.description: A new China-linked cybercrime group known as TA4922 has expanded its targeting focus to target European organizations in the U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa. These efforts have been complemented by a "rapid operational tempo" and a continually evolving malware arsenal comprising known families like ValleyRAT (aka Winos 4.0) and Atlas RAT (aka AtlasCross RAT), as well as previously

items.title: Mirasvit Vulnerability Exploited to Execute Code on Magento Servers
items.description: A flaw in the Full Page Cache Warmer extension can be exploited without authentication via serialized PHP object payloads. The post Mirasvit Vulnerability Exploited to Execute Code on Magento Servers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chinese Cybercrime Group in Spotlight for Record Campaign Pace
items.description: Relying on social engineering, the hacking group engages in credential phishing, malware distribution, and fraud activities. The post Chinese Cybercrime Group in Spotlight for Record Campaign Pace appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FlutterShell Backdoor Spreads to macOS via Malicious Google and YouTube Ads
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a macOS malvertising campaign codenamed Operation FlutterBridge that spreads a new backdoor called FlutterShell. According to Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the campaign is said to be the next stage of a previously reported activity cluster dubbed JSCoreRunner (aka FileRipple) in late August 2025. The cybercrime group behind the two attack chains is

items.title: Cisco warns of critical Unified CM flaw with PoC exploit code
items.description: Cisco has released security updates to patch a critical-severity Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) flaw that allows attackers to gain root privileges. [...]

items.title: Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown
items.description: Law enforcement and tech companies disrupted infrastructure linked to scammers operating across Southeast Asia. The post Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects to funnel unsuspecting users through a Traffic Distribution System (TDS) and deliver malware families like Remus Stealer, AnimateClipper, and the SessionGate framework. "The sites are well-designed and often look like legitimate project portals at a glance, sometimes referencing

items.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
items.description: Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it through Dropbox and OneDrive so the traffic blended into normal cloud activity. Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team reported the campaign this week. This points to espionage, not a money grab:

items.title: Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability
items.description: The high-severity flaw can be exploited remotely, without authentication, in server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks. The post Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft
items.description: A researcher has disclosed the full details of the vulnerability and released a PoC without notifying Microsoft in advance. The post VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft's Coreutils for Windows, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;ve been using the GnuWin32 CoreUtils for Windows for many years now (it gives you many *nix core commands on Windows).

items.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
items.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrency fraud targeting Americans. The "Disruption Week" operation began May 18, 2026, leading to the takedown of millions of social media, email, and internet access accounts used by transnational

items.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
items.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
items.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.

items.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
items.description: A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, fake a message from their boss, push the phone into a Zoom call, or quietly poison its long-term memory. No malicious app on the phone is required. The assistant just had to treat a hostile

items.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
items.description: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

items.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
items.description: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data.

items.title: Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform
items.description: Coralogix offers a full-stack observability platform that unifies logs, metrics, traces, security, and AI observability. The post Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Continuing Scans for swagger.json, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: Enterprise applications often still use complex standards like SOAP for web services. The big advantage of SOAP is its tight and extensive standards, which enable interoperability across an enterprise governed by web services. The disadvantage of SOAP: First, while it is de facto usually used over HTTP, it does not leverage HTTP, leading to unnecessary complexity. Secondly, kids don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t RTFM, and developers these days tend not to appreciate the art of careful system design; they rather throw code at an IDE to see what sticks, if they don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t vibe code it anyway.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.description: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 29th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9950, (Fri, May 29th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
